steps.title: Spot the missing auxiliary verb
steps.content: The word “considered” is a past participle with no preceding auxiliary verb. In “yellow ______ considered complementary to blue,” a finite auxiliary verb is needed before the participle to form a complete main clause.

steps.title: Apply the passive voice rule
steps.content: The passive voice requires a finite form of “to be” + a past participle. “Is” provides this, creating “is considered.” The other choices—”having been,” “to be,” and “being”—are nonfinite verb forms that cannot function as a main verb. That matches choice A — A is correct.
